items.text:
Campus Mailroom Holiday Hours
Effective: December 20 to January 2
Location: Student Union, Room 105

Please be advised that the campus mailroom will operate on a reduced schedule during the winter break. Regular semester hours (9:00 AM to 5:00 PM, Monday-Friday) will resume on January 3.

Holiday Schedule:
• December 20-23: 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM
• December 24-27: CLOSED
• December 28-30: 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM
• December 31-January 2: CLOSED

Important Reminders:
• Package pick-up requires a valid student ID.
• A $5.00 late fee will be charged for packages held over 30 days.
• Mail forwarding must be requested online via the student portal by December 15.

For questions, contact mailroom@university.edu or call (555) 123-4567.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what must a student do to pick up a package?

options.A: Pay a $5.00 late fee in advance.
options.B: Request mail forwarding by December 15.
options.C: Present a valid student identification card.
options.D: Visit the mailroom during regular semester hours.

questions.explanation: The notice states 'Package pick-up requires a valid student ID.'

questions.stem: The word 'resume' in the notice is closest in meaning to

options.A: begin again
options.B: summarize
options.C: shorten
options.D: cancel

questions.explanation: In the context of hours restarting after a break, 'resume' means to begin again.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the mailroom's schedule on December 26?

options.A: It will be open from 11:00 AM to 1:00 PM.
options.B: It will not be open for service.
options.C: It will operate from 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM.
options.D: It will charge a late fee for package pick-up.

questions.explanation: The notice lists December 24-27 as CLOSED, so December 26 is included in that closure period.

items.text:
Laundry Room Renovation – Anderson Hall
Dates: March 12 – April 2, 2025

Please be informed that the laundry facilities in Anderson Hall basement will be temporarily closed for renovation starting Monday, March 12. The project is scheduled for completion by Wednesday, April 2. During this period, residents of Anderson Hall may use the laundry rooms in the following locations:
• Carter Hall (first floor): 6 washers, 8 dryers
• University Commons (lower level): 10 washers, 12 dryers

Key Information:
• Access to alternate locations requires your student ID card.
• Machine rates remain $2.00 per wash and $1.50 per dry cycle.
• The renovated Anderson Hall laundry room will feature new, energy-efficient machines.
• For maintenance requests at alternate sites, please contact Facilities at ext. 5555.

We appreciate your patience during these improvements.

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the cost to dry one load of laundry during the renovation?

options.A: It requires a special access fee.
options.B: It is included with the wash cost.
options.C: It is one dollar and fifty cents.
options.D: It is two dollars per cycle.

questions.explanation: The notice states machine rates include '$1.50 per dry cycle.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the laundry facilities in Carter Hall?

options.A: They are located on the lower level.
options.B: They will also be renovated in April.
options.C: They have fewer dryers than washers.
options.D: They require a separate access fee.

questions.explanation: By comparing the listed machine counts for Carter Hall (6 washers, 8 dryers) and University Commons (10 washers, 12 dryers), one can infer they have different numbers of machines.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce a price increase for laundry services.
options.B: To inform residents of a temporary closure and alternative options.
options.C: To describe new energy-efficient laundry machines.
options.D: To request that residents avoid using laundry facilities.

questions.explanation: The primary purpose is to announce the renovation closure (March 12-April 2) and provide alternative laundry locations.

items.text:
Temporary Closure of West Quad Laundry Room
Dates: October 15 – November 5

Notice is hereby given that the laundry room in West Quad Building D will be closed for essential plumbing repairs from Monday, October 15 through Tuesday, November 5. All other laundry rooms on campus will remain open during their regular hours.

Alternative Facilities:
• East Quad Laundry Room (Building A, Level 1): Open 7 AM–11 PM
• Student Center Laundry (Annex Room 12): Open 24 hours

Important Guidelines:
• Students must use their campus ID for access to alternative locations.
• A $10.00 refundable deposit is required to obtain a temporary access card for the Student Center laundry, if needed. Deposit refunds are processed at the Campus Services office.
• Report any issues with machines immediately to the Facilities hotline at 555-0100.

We apologize for the inconvenience. The upgraded system will improve service reliability.

format_metadata.title: Temporary Closure of West Quad Laundry Room

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is required to use the East Quad laundry room?

options.A: A $10.00 refundable deposit.
options.B: A report to the Facilities hotline.
options.C: Attendance during specific repair dates.
options.D: A valid campus identification card.

questions.explanation: The notice states 'Students must use their campus ID for access to alternative locations,' which includes East Quad.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the Student Center laundry facility?

options.A: It is the only alternative with 24-hour access.
options.B: It requires a reservation in advance.
options.C: It is located in Building D of West Quad.
options.D: It is closed during plumbing repairs.

questions.explanation: The notice lists Student Center Laundry as 'Open 24 hours,' while East Quad has hours '7 AM–11 PM.' Thus, it can be inferred the Student Center is the only listed alternative with 24-hour access.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To announce a new 24-hour laundry facility.
options.B: To notify residents of a closure and provide alternative arrangements.
options.C: To explain how to obtain a refund for laundry deposits.
options.D: To describe upcoming plumbing upgrades across campus.

questions.explanation: The notice primarily informs about the West Quad laundry room closure (Oct 15-Nov 5) and lists alternative locations and related guidelines.

items.text:
Subject: Action Required: Fall Semester Parking Permit Renewal

Dear Student,

This is a reminder that fall semester parking permits expire on August 31. To avoid a $45.00 late fee, you must renew your permit online through the Campus Portal by 5:00 PM on August 25.

Renewal Instructions:
1. Log in to the Campus Portal using your student credentials.
2. Navigate to the 'Vehicle Services' section.
3. Select 'Permit Renewal' and confirm your vehicle information.
4. Pay the $120.00 permit fee. Payment can be made by credit/debit card or electronic check.

Your new permit will be available for pick-up at the Parking Office (Smith Hall, Room 200) starting September 4. You'll need to show your student ID and vehicle registration. If you no longer need a parking permit, please update your status in the Portal to avoid further communications.

We're trying to streamline the process, so don't hesitate to contact us at parking@university.edu with questions.

Best regards,

Campus Parking Services

format_metadata.subject: Action Required: Fall Semester Parking Permit Renewal

questions.stem: According to the email, what must a student do to avoid a late fee?

options.A: Pick up the new permit by September 4.
options.B: Pay the permit fee with a credit card only.
options.C: Update their vehicle information in person.
options.D: Renew the permit online by August 25.

questions.explanation: The email states 'to avoid a $45.00 late fee, you must renew your permit online... by 5:00 PM on August 25.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the Parking Office?

options.A: It is located in the Campus Portal.
options.B: It is closed for renovations in September.
options.C: It requires an appointment for permit pick-up.
options.D: It will not issue permits before September 4.

questions.explanation: The email states the new permit 'will be available for pick-up... starting September 4,' implying they will not be available before that date.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To announce an increase in parking permit fees.
options.B: To instruct students on renewing their parking permits before a deadline.
options.C: To provide the contact information for the Parking Office.
options.D: To explain how to update vehicle registration details.

questions.explanation: The email's primary purpose is to remind students of the expiration date and provide instructions and a deadline for renewal to avoid a late fee.

items.text:
Subject: Your Campus Parking Permit

Hi,

We've noticed your parking permit for Lot B is set to expire on May 15. You can renew it anytime between now and May 14 through the online portal. There's a $5.00 discount if you renew by May 1!

To complete your renewal:
• Log into the student portal and go to 'Campus Services.'
• Click 'Parking' and follow the prompts.
• The standard permit fee is $85.00 per semester.
• You'll receive a confirmation email with a digital permit you can print or display on your phone.

If you don't renew by May 15, your vehicle may be subject to ticketing. Also, remember that overnight parking rules are still enforced during the summer session. If you have any issues with the portal, just reply to this email or call us at (555) 789-0123.

Thanks,

Maria Chen
Parking Administration

questions.stem: According to the email, what is the standard fee for a semester parking permit?

options.A: Eighty-five dollars.
options.B: Five dollars with a discount.
options.C: One hundred twenty dollars.
options.D: Forty-five dollars after May 1.

questions.explanation: The email states 'The standard permit fee is $85.00 per semester.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the renewal process described in the email?

options.A: It must be completed in person at an office.
options.B: It offers a financial incentive for early action.
options.C: It is only available to students living on campus.
options.D: It requires a vehicle inspection first.

questions.explanation: The email mentions a '$5.00 discount if you renew by May 1,' which is a financial incentive for renewing early.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To warn a student about an upcoming parking ticket.
options.B: To introduce new overnight parking rules.
options.C: To provide technical support for the student portal.
options.D: To remind a permit holder about renewal options and deadlines.

questions.explanation: The email primarily serves as a reminder that the permit expires May 15, provides renewal instructions, mentions a discount, and warns of consequences for not renewing.

items.text:
BIOL 101: Introduction to Biology – Lab Component
Grading Breakdown:
• Lab Reports (10 total): 40% of final grade
• Midterm Practical Exam: 25%
• Final Practical Exam: 25%
• Participation & Attendance: 10%

Key Policies:
1. Lab reports are due at the start of the lab session one week after the experiment. Late reports will be penalized 10% per day, up to a maximum of 50%.
2. Attendance is mandatory. More than two unexcused absences will result in a full letter grade reduction.
3. Safety goggles and lab coats must be worn at all times in the laboratory. Failure to comply will result in dismissal from the session.

Important Dates:
• Midterm Practical: October 17, 2025 (during lab hours)
• Final Practical: December 12, 2025 (during lab hours)
• Last day to submit all lab reports: December 5, 2025

format_metadata.course_title: BIOL 101: Introduction to Biology – Lab Component

questions.stem: According to the syllabus, what is the consequence of missing more than two unexcused lab sessions?

options.A: A 10% penalty on the final grade.
options.B: Automatic failure of the lab component.
options.C: A reduction of one full letter grade.
options.D: Required completion of extra lab reports.

questions.explanation: The syllabus states 'More than two unexcused absences will result in a full letter grade reduction.'

options.A: They are the most heavily weighted component of the grade.
options.B: They cannot be submitted after December 5.
options.C: They are due on the same day as the experiment.
options.D: They are graded solely on participation.

questions.explanation: The syllabus lists 'Last day to submit all lab reports: December 5, 2025,' implying they will not be accepted after that date.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this syllabus excerpt?

options.A: To list the required textbooks for BIOL 101.
options.B: To describe the experiments performed in the lab.
options.C: To outline the grading structure and key policies for the lab.
options.D: To provide contact information for the lab instructor.

questions.explanation: The excerpt primarily presents the grading breakdown, key policies (late work, attendance, safety), and important dates for the lab component.

items.text:
@CampusDevTeam
Just launched the first version of 'CampusNav' – an app built by students for students! 🎉

It started as a class project last semester, but we kept working on it because we got so many requests for better campus maps. Now you can find the shortest route between classes, see real-time study room occupancy, and even get alerts for shuttle buses!

The best part? It's completely free and doesn't show ads. We're just passionate about making campus life easier. Give it a try and let us know what you think! Download links in our bio.

#CampusTech #StudentProject #UniversityLife

questions.stem: According to the post, what was the original inspiration for the CampusNav app?

options.A: A need for real-time shuttle alerts.
options.B: Requests for a paid navigation service.
options.C: A project for an academic course.
options.D: A desire to display advertisements.

questions.explanation: The post states 'It started as a class project last semester.'

options.A: To announce the release of a new student-built app.
options.B: To recruit new members for a development team.
options.C: To complain about the existing campus map system.
options.D: To provide a tutorial on using study room occupancy features.

questions.explanation: The post begins with 'Just launched the first version of 'CampusNav'' and encourages followers to try it, making announcement its primary purpose.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the developers of the CampusNav app?

options.A: They are professional software engineers.
options.B: They plan to charge for the app in the future.
options.C: They are required to complete it for a grade.
options.D: They are motivated by improving student experience.

questions.explanation: The post states 'We're just passionate about making campus life easier,' implying their motivation is improving student experience, not grades or profit.

items.text:
Updated Lost and Found Policy – Campus Safety Office
Effective: January 16, 2025

To improve item recovery, the Campus Safety Office has updated its lost and found procedures. All found items should now be delivered directly to the Safety Office in Garner Hall, Room 101, during business hours (8:00 AM to 6:00 PM, Monday-Friday).

Key Changes:
• Items will be logged and stored for 90 days (previously 60 days).
• High-value items (electronics, wallets, keys) will be secured in locked storage.
• Claimants must provide a detailed description and show photo ID.
• A $2.00 administrative fee will be charged for reclaiming any item, payable at the time of pickup. This fee supports the maintenance of the lost and found system.

Unclaimed items will be donated or disposed of after the holding period. For questions, please call (555) 234-5678.

format_metadata.title: Updated Lost and Found Policy – Campus Safety Office

questions.stem: According to the notice, what is the new holding period for lost items?

options.A: Thirty days from the date found.
options.B: Sixty days as per the previous policy.
options.C: During business hours only.
options.D: Ninety days after being logged.

questions.explanation: The notice states 'Items will be logged and stored for 90 days (previously 60 days).'

questions.stem: What can be inferred about the $2.00 fee mentioned in the notice?

options.A: It is charged when reporting a lost item.
options.B: It is waived for high-value items like electronics.
options.C: It is a penalty for losing items frequently.
options.D: It is collected when a person retrieves their item.

questions.explanation: The notice states the fee is 'charged for reclaiming any item, payable at the time of pickup,' implying it is collected upon retrieval.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this notice?

options.A: To inform the campus community of changes to lost and found procedures.
options.B: To announce the relocation of the Campus Safety Office.
options.C: To warn students about an increase in lost item incidents.
options.D: To list the business hours for Garner Hall.

questions.explanation: The notice announces 'updated its lost and found procedures' and details key changes like holding period, fee, and drop-off location.
